name: Electric Field from Point Charges
description: Calculate electric field magnitude and direction due to one or more point charges using superposition principle
identification_signal: Questions asking for electric field at a point due to point charges, or net field from multiple charges

strategy.step_1: Calculate magnitude of each contribution: E = k|Q|/r²
strategy.step_2: Determine direction: away from positive, toward negative
strategy.step_3: Break into components if needed (2D/3D problems)
strategy.step_4: Add vectorially to find net field

front.text: Find the electric field 0.5 m from a +3 μC charge

back.title: Point Charge Field
back.explanation: Use E = kQ/r². Direction: radially outward from positive charge.

spot_it.instructions: Identify problems requiring electric field calculation vs electric force

items.prompt_latex: Find E-field at point P due to charge Q
Find force on charge q in E-field
items.explanation: Use F = qE, not this pattern

steps.title: Source vs Test Charge
steps.instruction: Identify which charges CREATE the field (source Q), not any test charge
steps.why: E-field exists independent of test charges
common_errors: Using test charge q instead of source charge Q

steps.title: Direction Convention
steps.instruction: Positive source: field points AWAY. Negative source: field points TOWARD.
steps.why: E-field direction is defined as force on positive test charge

problem_cards.prompt_latex: Q_1 = +2\mu\text{C at } x=0, Q_2 = -4\mu\text{C at } x=0.3\text{m}. \text{ Find } E \text{ at } x=0.6\text{m}

steps: E₁ from Q₁: k(2μC)/(0.6)² in +x direction (away from +)
E₂ from Q₂: k(4μC)/(0.3)² in +x direction (toward -)
solution.verification: Both fields point right; negative charge attracts positive test charge

what_mastery_means: Never confuses source and test charge
